samples.gvd:
module pulse_detect(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input data_in,
    output reg data_out
);

reg [1:0] state;
reg [1:0] next_state;
parameter IDLE = 2'b00, PULSE = 2'b01, WAIT = 2'b10;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        state <= IDLE;
    end else begin
        state <= next_state;
    end
end

always @(*) begin
    case (state)
        IDLE: begin
            if (data_in) begin
                next_state = PULSE;
            end else begin
                next_state = IDLE;
            end
        end
        PULSE: begin
            if (data_in) begin
                next_state = WAIT;
            end else begin
                next_state = IDLE;
            end
        end
        WAIT: begin
            if (data_in) begin
                next_state = WAIT;
            end else begin
                next_state = IDLE;
            end
        end
        default: begin
            next_state = IDLE;
        end
    endcase
end

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        data_out <= 1'b0;
    end else begin
        case (next_state)
            PULSE: begin
                data_out <= 1'b1;
            end
            default: begin
                data_out <= 1'b0;
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for pulse detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </input>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the pulse detection process.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State indicating the end of a pulse.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <para>Declare a register state to keep track of the current state of the pulse detection process.</para>
    <para>Declare a register pre_data to store the previous value of data_in.</para>
    <para>Initialize state and pre_data in the initial block.</para>
    <para>Inside an always block, sensitive to the positive edge of the clk signal and the negative edge of the rst_n signal, implement the logic for pulse detection and output generation.</para>
    <para>In the reset condition (~rst_n), set the state register to the initial state and set the data_out output to 0, indicating no pulse.</para>
    <para>Continue the detection process for the remaining states. For each state, check the value of data_in and transition to the next state accordingly.</para>
    <para>If the current state satisfies the last state of a "pulse", set data_out to 1, indicating the end of a pulse.</para>
    <para>Otherwise, set data_out to 0.</para>
    </implementation>
</module>
